Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine in both serum and blood, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, further supporting possible renal dysfunction or nephrotic syndrome. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation for possible chronic kidney disease and further assessment of proteinuria and renal function.
